require "set"

struct Point
  x, y, z : Int32
end

def points_to_set(points : Array(Array(Int64))) : Set(Point)
  set = Set(Point)
  points.each do |p_arr|
    set.add(Point(p_arr[0].to_int32, p_arr[1].to_int32, p_arr[2].to_int32))
  end
  set
end

def array_to_point(arr : Array(Int64)) : Point
  Point(arr[0].to_int32, arr[1].to_int32, arr[2].to_int32)
end

  target_point = array_to_point(target)

  # Check generation 0
  initial_points_set = points_to_set(points)
  if initial_points_set.includes?(target_point)
    return 0
  end

          end

      return k
    end

    # Update the set of available points for the next generation
    # The next generation's points are the union of points from generation 0 to k
    # Since we are iterating k=1, 2, 3..., we need to check if any new unique points were created.
